task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris application window is open and displaying the main menu.
The Science/Experiment section is active (often represented by a microscope or similar icon), displaying various science-related sub-activities.
The 'Mixing paint colors' activity screen is visible, displaying paint sources (typically Cyan, Magenta, Yellow), a mixing area, and a specific target color to match.

task_completion.description: Mix the available colors in the mixing area
task_completion.visual_evidence: Paints have been dispensed or combined in the mixing area, showing a change in color from the default empty state as an attempt to match the target.

task_completion.description: Complete at least one color-matching exercise successfully
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success indicator (such as a smiley face, stars, a 'Good!' message, or a level-complete animation) is visible, or the interface has advanced to a new target color, proving the mixed color successfully matched the target.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the GCompris GUI rather than using terminal commands or scripts to bypass the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: Cursor movements, clicks, or active UI states are visible within the GCompris graphical interface.

integrity.description: Agent used the actual GCompris application
integrity.visual_evidence: The distinctive cartoonish, educational graphical interface of GCompris is visible, not a text editor, web browser, or mock-up.

integrity.description: The color mixing and success sequence appears genuine
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a logical sequence of adding individual colors to the mix before the success indicator appears, without sudden unexplained jumps directly to a success screen.

privileged_info_for_vlm: GCompris is a real educational software suite. The 'Mixing paint colors' activity is located in the Science/Experiment category. It uses a subtractive color mixing model (Cyan, Magenta, Yellow). Success in an exercise is typically indicated by a visual reward (e.g., a smiley face, stars, or an animation) and progression to a new target color.
